si.t: Fewer Critical Incidents caused by change Customer Satisfaction
Change Management Provider Management Event Management Continuity Management Alignment for Leoni Standards
Mean elapsed time for incident resolution
Average time to achieve incident resolution, broken down by Priority
This is one of the key drivers of customer satisfaction for support groups Target is to reduce the mean elapsed time for incident resolution
KPI has been updated to retired because it is the same as INC-12 (resolution time)
[Incident] [State] IS "Assigned" OR "New" OR "In Progress" OR "On Hold" OR "Reopened" OR "None" AND [State] is not one of "Resolved", "Closed", "Canceled"
Count of Incidents per state, Aging Report (Monthly Snapshot)
Backlog can provide information about capacity and focus.
Drill down by Components and Aging groups and not states
Percentage of incidents closed by the service desk
Percentage of incidents closed by the service desk without reference to other levels of support (often referred to as ‘first point of contact’)

SERVICE NOW 1.-In the personalized list you need to add "Reassigment count" column.
EXCEL FILE 1.-In the excel file you need to filter "responsible user" column to the SD agent (Lassad,Chackrouni and Salem). 2.-In the "Reassignment count" column filter to 0,1,2.
Efficiency of service and help to define the capacity and the workload of GSD agents.
LM - Retired on 03.24.2022, will be handled as part of global service desk KPIs INC-04 & INC-10)
Number of incidents resolved in 1 hour
Count of Incident resolved in less than or 1 Hour
Number of Incidents Created in a Month
Number of Incidents Created in Month (Created in the month as a control measure)
Ticket volume can help identify trends to help predict what may be driving higher or lower ticket volumes. With this data, you can schedule agents accordingly, considering high-volume periods
Size of current incident backlog by IT service
Size of current incident backlog for each IT service

r.t: IS ONE OF "Assigned, New, In Progress, On Hold, Reopened"

si.t: Count of active Incidents by Service
Number of major incidents by IT service
Count of Major Incidents / Total IT Services
Count of Overdue Incidents which reached the response SLA
Efficiency of service and help to define the capacity and the workload of Responsible support agents.
Capacity Management Alignment for Leoni Standards Provider Management
Number of tickets processed per service desk agent

Help to decide about the dispatching logic of Items in ServiceNow and enhance ServiceNow Users knowledge.
Maintain the correct data of the items in servicenow to have accurate information for better KPI calculation

r.t: : Business Service Schedule based on SLAs

si.t: Ensures that the service responsible group are attending to the service as required. Provides information about capacity and focus.
Change Management Provider Management Event Management Continuity Management Alignment for Leoni Standards

r.t: Is one Of "Incidents Resolved last month"

si.t: 1- Count the breached incidents by service according to the incident priority and criticality of the affected service component. 2- Calculate the Average of the percentage of actual incident resolution time
Provides information for resolution metrics/SLA's performance for incidents resolved in a proper time.
Incident First Response Time Compliance Rate

r.t: is one of " Incidents Opened last month"

si.t: 1- Count the Number of non-compliant Response Time Incidents of the last month 2- Count the Number of Incidents by Service of the last month 3- Average of Incident response time per service
Average handle time at both an agent level and an organization level are able to get the right resource working on an incident
Count of Incident resolved in a Month
Provides trending according to the backlog, and new tickets
Count of Overdue Incidents which reached the resolution SLA
KPI will be drill down by Components and Aging groups and not states
Percentage of Closed Requests by Service Desk
Monthly Percentage of Closed Requests by Service Desk
Percentage of Request closed by Global Service Desk
Retired, will be handled as part of global service desk KPIs)
Number of Requests Created in Month
Number of Requests Created in Month (Created in the month as a control measure)
Count of Request created in Monthly
Ticket volume can help identify trends to help predict what may be driving higher or lower ticket volumes. With this data, you can schedule agents accordingly, considering high-volume periods.
Number of INC Converted to RITM
Number of incidents related to request fullfilment (wrongly created Incidents)
Count of Incident related to Request Fulfilment
Provides an overview of compliance of process adherence.
Only provides information if the support agents are transforming tickets to correct type using the buttons on the form.
The size of current backlog of requests
The size of current backlog of outstanding service requests

r.t: IS ONE OF "Assigned, On Hold,Work In Progress"

si.t: Count of Request, Aging Report (Monthly Snapshot)
Number of closed service request per Month
Number of Overdue changes by Month
Efficiency of service and help to define the capacity and the workload of Responsible support agent.
Number of critical changes by Month
Critical Changes indicat the number of changes in which remediation is required.
Number of Emergency Changes per Month
Critical Changes provides trending on how incidents attributable to changes indicates how well Change Management is used
Count of Changes created per Month
Ticket volume can help identify trends to help predict what may be driving higher or lower ticket volumes. With this data, you can schedule agents accordingly.
Number of Closed Changes per Month
Count of Changes closed per Month
Provides information on changes closed in a proper time.
Number of Open Changes per Month
Backlog can provide information about capacity and focus
Number of Problems Created in a Month

r.t: Is not one of "Risk Accepted", "Canceled", "Duplicate" Or

si.t:
[Number of high priority problems closed during the month] / [Total number of problems closed during the last month]
High priority problems include priority 1
Helps to avoid service interuption caused by incidents
Average Time to Close a Problem
Average time to close a problem

[Sum of the duration of all problems closed during the month] / [Total number of problems closed during the last month]
Duration is the length of time from creating to closure.
Helps to measure the improvement effort in Root cause Analysis. Provides information for resolution metrics/SLA's performance for Problem resolved in a proper time.
Indicates whether an effective Problem Management process is in place
Number of Articles Published in a Month
Number of Published Articles by Month and still published
[Total number of published Knowledge Articles during the last month]
Indicates the optimal knowledge growth within the organization
Should be a % of total to reach high effectiveness add category in the graphics
Number of Articles Created in a Month
[Total number of created Knowledge Articles during the last month]
Indicates the optimal knowledge growth within the organization and monitor outdated/duplicate information in the knowledge base
Number of Flagged Articles By Month
To raise the rating, could we instead look at how many times an article is read?
Number of Rated Articles By Month
Time to publish and article in the Month
Average of the [published] date - [created] date consider the published date for not KA is today
Indicates the performace to publish an article since creation
Percentage of Known Error Articles created By month
[Total number of created Known errors Knowledge Articles during the last month/ Total number of created Knowledge Articles during the last month] x 100
A review on the Error Knowledge template should be in place More information about the benefit of this KPI
Number of Outdated and retired Articles
Process owner involvement frequency of reporting this KPI
Number of Viewed Articles By Month
Process Owner involvment think to have the opposite ( What are the benefits to know the viewed articles)
Number of Needed Articles not available By Month
Process owner involvement Service Component Owner involvement
Number of Open Articles in the Month (backlog)
Drill down by Business Service (full category column)
Number of Tickets Closed by Service Desk
Number of Tickets Closed by Service Desk in Month

r.t: is "Requested Item" OR "Incident" OR "Change Request" OR "Change Task" OR "Problem"

si.t: [Total number of Tickets closed by Service Desk during the last month]
Drill down by Service Desk Agent
Number of tickets dispatched by Global Service Desk
Metric for the rest of the tables "Assigment to groups"
IA - Know the average answer time quantifies the speed at which a support agent dispach the tickets.
IA - we can use the existing metric "Average Dispatching duration" in Global Service Desk Dashboard.
IA - These metric will give you a good idea of team capacity and will provide you with a more detailed evaluation of the performance of Service Desk Agents ( from time and efficiency perspective)
IA - we can use the metric view to calculate this metric
Number of tickets wrongly assigned (Repeat Visits)
Provides an overview how well the dispaching logic is performing and if onboarding/training should be improved.
IA - Improve the support interactions and services and help to design the communication and strategies the best they can be
IA - We need to find a way to calculate this metric ( may be Surveys, additional fields tobe filled before closing a ticket)
Percentage of Viewed Articles By Month
Number Of rated Articles By Month
Average Time to Publish an Article

REQ-01 will be combined with REQ-05 and REQ-05 wont be calculated, add the current size graphic in the REQ-01
Number of open incidents at each stage (e.g. Assigned, In Progress, etc.)

Not relevant any more as it is covered by the KPI Inc-06
01.24.2022 The INC-03 is active again due the INC-06 wasn’t related to currect backlog, is related to incident created in a month, name has been changed from Total number of incidents to Number of Incidents created in a month

r.t: Updating the calculation logic (old one marked in Red, new ones in Green) [Incident]
[Active] IS "False" AND [Security relevant incident] IS "False" AND [Number] DOES NOT CONTAIN Task" AND

01.24.2022 The REQ-03 wasn’t related to currect backlog, is related to request created in a month, name has been changed from Total number of request to Number of request created in a month
Number of open Incident per stage
name changed to standardize naming for common KPIs : new name Number of open incidents (backlog)
Number of open service requests per stage
name changed to standardize naming for common KPIs : new name Number of open service requests (backlog)

Target has been updated from 390 to 331
Target has been updated from 1405 to 1615

Target has been updated from 5 to 64

ServiceNow Filter has been updated From

si.t: Target has been updated from >1028 to <1028

Name has been updated from Number of Overdue changes to Number of Past due Changes
Calculation logic changed, same filter is used but we will consider all past due changes
Acording to KPI committe, chart 2 (Ticket closed by Service Desk Agent) has to me removed

r.t: In order to continue measuring response task SLAs,
from "Number of Overdue Incidents" to "Number of Response Overdue Incidents",

from [Task SLAs] [SLA definition] DOES NOT CONTAIN "Reminder" [Business elapsed percentage] GREATER THAN "100" [Created] ON "Last Month"
To [Task SLAs]&[Incident] [SLA definition] DOES NOT CONTAIN "Reminder" [Business elapsed percentage] GREATER THAN "100" [Created] ON "Last Month" [SLA definition.Target] is "Response"

si.t: Responsible has been updated from Erik to Sheila
Target has been updated from 624 to 400 starting on January 2023
Target has been updated from 331 to 245 starting on January 2023
Target has been updated from 1615 to 1704 starting on January 2023
Target has been updated from 4337 to 2859 starting on January 2023
Target has been updated from 9121 to 11115 starting on January 2023
Target has been updated from 25 to 33 starting on January 2023

Global Calculation has been updated, currently the global calculation is done based on the current results of the month, Global calculation is now calculated based on the last 12 month average, current montly calculation wont be affected and will be displayed in the graphics in order to not loose visibility of the results, New Calculation approach will start on January 2023

Global Calculation has been updated, currently the global calculation is done based on the current results of the month, Global calculation is now calculated based on the last 12 month average, current montly calculation wont be affected and will be displayed in the graphics in order to not loose visibility of the results, New Calculation approach will start on January 2023 Calculation for current Month has been update from Ʃ of %-values of all service components / total number of service components x 100% To Ʃ total service component time – Ʃ total component downtime) / Ʃ total service component time x 100

Global Calculation has been updated, currently the global calculation is done based on the current results of the month, Global calculation is now calculated based on the last 12 month average, current montly calculation wont be affected and will be displayed in the graphics in order to not loose visibility of the results, New Calculation approach will start on January 2023 Calculation for current Month has been update from Ʃ of %-values of all service components with incidents / total number of components with incidents x 100% To Ʃ all incidents - Ʃ all non-compliant incidents) / Ʃ all incidents x 100%

Global Calculation has been updated, currently the global calculation is done based on the current results of the month, Global calculation is now calculated based on the last 12 month average, current montly calculation wont be affected and will be displayed in the graphics in order to not loose visibility of the results, New Calculation approach will start on January 2023 Calculation for current Month has been update from Ʃ of %-values of all service components with incidents / total number of components with incidents x 100% To (Ʃ all incidents - Ʃ all non-compliant incidents) / Ʃ all incidents x 100%
The name of the INC-09 KPI has been updated from Number of Response Overdue Incidents to Number of Past due Incidents (Response)
The name of the INC-15 KPI has been updated from Number of Resolution Overdue Incidents to Number of Past due Incidents (Resolution)
ServiceNow Filter has been updated, Incident must have only 1 Resolution task SLA attached, conditional added to the filted to avoid cancelled Task SLAs New contitional: [Stage] is not "Cancelled"
Added Departmental KPIs to keep monthly actuals and 12 month average measurements separate
Number of Incidents Related to Request Fullfilment
KPI Name has been update from Number of Incidents Related to Request Fullfilment to Number of INC Converted to RITM
Starting on March Sheila has been assigned as responsible for calculation, Responsible has been updated from Luis to Sheila

Starting on March Sheila has been assigned as responsible for calculation, Responsible has been updated from Lassaad to Sheila

Starting on March Khrystyna has been assigned as responsible for calculation, Responsible has been updated from Salem to Karen
Starting on March Sheila has been assigned as responsible for calculation, Responsible has been updated from Amal to Sheila
Starting on March Sheila has been assigned as responsible for calculation, Responsible has been updated from Amel to Sheila
Time to close a problem has been updated, Business duration is used instead of duration, 2023 results have been updated
